blurb:
Canton Cooks on Roswell Road is a solid neighborhood spot for Chinese food that goes a little beyond the usual takeout menu. The vibe is casual and relaxed, and it's a good call for a family dinner or a low-key weeknight meal when you want something different.

They're known for hot pot, lobster in black bean sauce, and BBQ duck. If you've never tried the lobster in black bean sauce, it's the kind of dish that makes you wonder why you haven't been coming here all along.

They open at noon on weekends, so it's a good option for a Saturday or Sunday lunch if you're in the area.
address: 5984 Roswell Rd NE, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Saturday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM, Sunday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM

restaurant_name: Mister O1 Extraordinary Pizza Sandy Springs
blurb:
Almost 5,500 reviews at 4.9 stars should tell you something. Mister O1 is legitimately one of the best pizza spots in the area, and it's right here in Sandy Springs off Blue Stone Road. The thin-crust Italian style is the real deal, made with ingredients either sourced locally or brought in from Italy.

If you haven't been, start with one of their signature pizzas and add a salad or antipasto on the side. The portions are solid and the quality is noticeably a step above your typical pizza night.

Open seven days a week starting at 11:30am, so it works for lunch, dinner, or a late Friday or Saturday night out. Good for families, date nights, or a casual group dinner.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Ste 240, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Thursday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Friday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Saturday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Sunday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM

blurb:
Hearth Pizza Tavern is the kind of place you bring people when you want a nicer pizza night without it feeling like a big production. The brick interior is warm and comfortable, they have a patio, and the beer and wine list is solid. Good for date night or dinner with another couple.

The pizzas are inventive, not your standard chain toppings. Worth browsing the menu ahead of time because there's more going on than you'd expect. The appetizers are worth ordering too, not just an afterthought.

They're open seven days a week and close at 9pm on Sundays, so keep that in mind if you're thinking about a weekend dinner.
address: 5992 Roswell Rd NE, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM

blurb:
BJ's is one of those places that works for just about any group because the menu is huge and the vibe is relaxed. It's a good call for a casual weeknight dinner, a group outing after work, or grabbing a late bite since they're open until midnight most nights and 1am on weekends.

The house brews are a big part of the appeal here. The pub fare is familiar and consistent, which is exactly what you want when half the group can't agree on what they're in the mood for. It's not going to blow your mind, but it's reliably good and the portions are generous.

Parking is easy and it's right off Stemmons, so it's convenient if you're coming from most parts of Lewisville.
address: 2609 S Stemmons Fwy, Lewisville, TX 75067, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 1:00 AM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 1:00 AM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM

blurb:
Thrill is the most fun dinner experience in Sandy Springs, full stop. It's a big, two-level restaurant with all-you-can-eat Korean BBQ, handcrafted cocktails, and a bar scene that goes until 2am on weekends. Great for a group birthday, a night out with friends, or any time you want dinner to feel like an event.

The all-you-can-eat Korean BBQ is the main draw. You cook the meat right at your table, and the experience alone makes it worth going. The cocktails are taken seriously here too, not just an afterthought.

Dinner only Monday through Thursday (opens at 5pm), but on Fridays and Saturdays they open at 11am and stay open until 2am. Worth planning around.
address: 5958 Roswell Rd, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 5:00 PM – 12:00 AM, Tuesday: 5:00 PM – 12:00 AM, Wednesday: 5:00 PM – 12:00 AM, Thursday: 5:00 PM – 12:00 AM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM

blurb:
If you're looking for a special occasion spot that actually lives up to the price tag, Canoe is the one. It sits right on the Chattahoochee River, and the garden setting is genuinely beautiful, not just a marketing line. This is the kind of place you take out-of-town guests or celebrate something that deserves more than a nice chain.

The menu changes with the seasons since they source locally, so what's on offer shifts, but the kitchen consistently turns out well-crafted New American dishes. It's farm-to-table done with real intention, not just a buzzword on a chalkboard.

They have a lunch service on weekdays and brunch on Sundays if you want the full experience without the dinner price. Reservations are strongly recommended, especially on weekends.
address: 4199 Paces Ferry Rd SE, Atlanta, GA 30339, USA

blurb:
If you've never tried Persian food, Yalda in Sandy Springs is the right place to start. It's a great spot for a girls night out or a dinner with friends who want something a little different. The atmosphere is warm and the service gets consistently high marks from people who've been.

The hummus and wings are a good way to kick things off, and the chicken shish kabobs served with rice with lentils and raisins are the kind of dish people talk about on the drive home. End with the tiramisu and thank me later.

They close between lunch and dinner (2:45 to 4pm), so plan around that if you're thinking about stopping by mid-afternoon.
address: 6500 Aria Blvd Suite 500, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
The Select has a cool Art Deco feel and an open kitchen, and it's the kind of place that works well for a date night or a nicer dinner out when you want something with a little more atmosphere than usual. It's in the same Blue Stone Road area as Mister O1, so the neighborhood is easy to get to.

The menu leans modern European, and the conservatory-style space makes the whole thing feel a bit more special than your average Sandy Springs dinner. It's a good choice when you want to impress someone without driving all the way into town.

They don't open until 4pm, so this is a dinner-only spot on most nights. Sunday brunch starts at 10am if you want to check it out on a weekend morning.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Suite 200, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 4:00 – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 4:00 – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 4:00 – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 4:00 – 11:00 PM, Friday: 4:00 PM – 12:00 AM, Saturday: 4:00 PM – 12:00 AM, Sunday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb:
Not a restaurant, but worth knowing about if you're looking for something to do with the family. Cinemark Lewisville has stadium seating and an XD screen for the bigger blockbusters, and the self-service kiosks make it pretty painless to grab tickets without standing in a long line.

It's a solid option for date night, a rainy Saturday with the kids, or killing a few hours on a holiday weekend. The 4.3 rating across nearly 2,700 reviews says people keep coming back.
address: 2401 S Stemmons Fwy, Lewisville, TX 75067, USA

blurb:
If you haven't been to Rreal Tacos yet, put it on your list. The place is lively and fun, and it works equally well for a casual weeknight dinner or a late night out with friends. They're open until midnight most nights, which is rare around here.

People rave about the Esquite to start, and for drinks the Pineapple Guava pitcher or the Passion Fruit pitcher are both worth getting for the table. The reviews consistently call them well-balanced and refreshing, not just sugary sweet.

With a 4.9 rating across more than 9,000 reviews, this place has earned its reputation. Go hungry and plan to stay a while.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM

blurb:
Brooklyn Cafe has been a Sandy Springs staple for a reason. It's warm, comfortable, and the kind of place where you can actually have a conversation. Good for a weeknight dinner out, a birthday, or any time you want a proper sit-down meal without driving far.

The menu is American bistro, done well. The wine list is broad, and the patio is a nice option when the weather cooperates. It draws a loyal crowd for a reason.

One thing to know: they are closed on Sundays. So if you're planning ahead for the week, aim for any other night.
address: 220 Sandy Springs Cir, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
If you're feeding picky eaters or a big group on a budget, Regal Buffet gets the job done. It's a roomy spot with a wide spread of Chinese dishes plus sushi rolls, so there's usually something for everyone at the table.

It's the kind of place that works well for a casual family lunch or a low-key dinner when you just want to eat a lot without spending a lot. The prices are low and the variety is the whole point.

They're open until 9:30 or 10pm most nights, so it's a reasonable option if dinner runs a little late.
address: 251 N Stemmons Fwy, Lewisville, TX 75067, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM

restaurant_name: Wu Wei Din Chinese Cuisine - Lewisville
blurb:
This one has built up a real following in Lewisville, and once you try it you'll understand why. Reviewers keep coming back and calling it a favorite, which says a lot. It's a good spot for a sit-down weeknight dinner or takeout when you want something better than the usual.

The orange chicken gets mentioned constantly and people say it's the best they've had. The pan fried potstickers, chicken udon noodles, and lo mein are also popular orders worth trying. The menu has plenty of options whether you're going solo or feeding the whole family.

Just note they close between lunch and dinner (3pm to 4pm) every day, so plan accordingly if you're thinking about a mid-afternoon meal.
address: 2505 S Stemmons Fwy #3480, Lewisville, TX 75067, USA

blurb:
Rosa's is one of those places you end up at more often than you planned because it's fast, cheap, and actually good. The housemade tortillas are the real draw here and they make a noticeable difference compared to your typical fast-casual Tex-Mex spot.

It's perfect for a quick family lunch, a last-minute dinner when nobody wants to cook, or even breakfast since they open at 6:30am every day. The prices are low enough that feeding a family of four doesn't feel like a splurge.

They're open late on weekends (until 11pm), which is a handy detail to keep in your back pocket.
address: 705 N Denton Tap Rd, Coppell, TX 75019, USA
hours: Monday: 6:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 6:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 6:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 6:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Friday: 6:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Saturday: 6:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Sunday: 6:30 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb:
Rreal Tacos in Sandy Springs has over 9,000 reviews at 4.9 stars, which is basically unheard of. The vibe is lively and fun, good for a group night out or just a casual weeknight when you want real Mexican food without a lot of effort.

Start with the Esquite if you want a crowd-pleaser right out of the gate. For drinks, the Pineapple Guava pitcher or the Passion Fruit pitcher are both solid choices, refreshing and well balanced without being too sweet. The food and the drinks together make this a full evening, not just a quick bite.

They're open until midnight most nights, so it also works well for a late dinner after a game or a movie.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
